design_philosophy: A highly technical, instrument-panel aesthetic mimicking the feel of an amateur radio transceiver. It leverages dense data layouts, monospace fonts for alignment, and amber phosphor glows against deep obsidian backgrounds.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: The 'Control Room' Grid - High density, clear hierarchy, every pixel utilized. Avoid soft empty spaces; instead, use structural borders to separate zones.

layout_structure.header: col-span-12 flex justify-between items-center border-b border-zinc-800 pb-4 mb-6

surface_strategies.cards: bg-[#121212] border border-zinc-800/80 rounded-none relative overflow-hidden
surface_strategies.inputs: bg-[#09090b] border border-zinc-700 focus:border-amber-500 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-amber-500 text-zinc-100 rounded-none font-mono text-sm
surface_strategies.buttons: bg-amber-500 hover:bg-amber-600 text-black font-bold uppercase tracking-wider rounded-none transition-all duration-200 shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(245,158,11,0.15)] hover:shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(245,158,11,0.3)]
surface_strategies.table_header: bg-[#1e1e24] border-b-2 border-zinc-700 text-xs uppercase tracking-widest text-zinc-400 py-3 px-4

header.style: Fixed or sticky top, solid background (#09090b) with a hard bottom border (#27272a). Features the App Name ('QSO LOG') in large Barlow Condensed, possibly an amber dot to signify 'ON AIR' or 'LIVE'.
header.navigation: Minimalist textual links with amber underline on active state.

qso_form.location: Sidebar (Right column on desktop, stacked on mobile).
qso_form.style: Dense form layout. Labels above inputs. Inputs must use IBM Plex Mono to resemble a digital readout. Use Shadcn components heavily customized to remove rounding (rounded-none).

qso_table.style: A raw, border-collapse table. No cards around individual rows. It should look like an endless terminal log. Use monospace font for the entire table body. Align 'Fréquence' to the right.
qso_table.actions: Hovering over a row reveals 'Edit' and 'Delete' icons (Phosphor Icons) in a subtle muted color, turning amber/red on hover.

stats_widget.style: Retro LED display vibe. A dark container with a prominent, oversized glowing amber number for 'Total QSOs'. 'text-5xl font-bold tracking-tighter text-amber-500'.

filters_search.style: Placed above the table. A large, full-width search bar for 'Indicatif' with a leading magnifying glass icon. Filter dropdowns for Date Range and Frequency Bands (e.g., 20m, 40m, 2m).

motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Hard cuts or fast transitions (0.15s). No slow, muddy fades.
motion_and_interactions.focus_states: Inputs gain a sharp 1px amber border and a subtle amber box-shadow. No soft blue rings.
motion_and_interactions.loading: An ASCII-style loader or a simple blinking amber block cursor (like a terminal terminal).

icons_and_assets.usage_rules: Use precise, technical icons. 'Broadcast' for frequency, 'CalendarBlank' for date, 'User' for name, 'IdentificationCard' for callsign.

images.description: Subtle, dark-mode abstract background representing radio frequencies, to be used with high opacity mask (e.g. 5-10%) behind the main container to give a 'radio shack' texture.
Minimalist vector icon of an amateur radio transmitter. Use this in the center of the table area when there are 0 QSOs logged, adding 'opacity-50' to blend it.

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: All text must pass WCAG AA against the #09090b background. The primary amber (#F59E0B) against black is highly legible.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install Google Fonts `Barlow Condensed` and `IBM Plex Mono` via Google Fonts CDN in index.html.
Do NOT use standard Shadcn rounded cards. Override Shadcn configurations with `rounded-none` or `rounded-sm` at most.
Implement the layout with a 12-column CSS Grid. The main list takes 8 columns, the form and stats take 4 columns on the right.
Use a pure dark theme (no light mode toggle needed as per user request). Background is #09090b.
For the background, apply the `hero_background` image URL via a fixed div with `opacity-5` or `opacity-10` and `mix-blend-screen` or `mix-blend-overlay` so it doesn't distract from the data.
Ensure the 'Fréquence' column in the table is right-aligned to make comparing decimal numbers easier.
Render the Add Form constantly visible on the side rather than hiding it behind a modal, to allow rapid data entry which is crucial for radio contests.
